explanation: This is the original command that matches the Sigma rule. It sets the SymlinkEvaluation behavior to R2L:1 using fsutil.
This is a variation of the original command that sets the SymlinkEvaluation behavior to R2R:1 using fsutil.

output: copy fsutil.exe not_harmful.exe && not_harmful.exe behavior set SymlinkEvaluation L2L:1 R2R:1
explanation: This command copies fsutil.exe to not_harmful.exe and executes it with the same behavior settings. It demonstrates a potential bypass by renaming the executable.
This command modifies the registry to set the SymlinkRemoteToLocalEvaluation value. It is an alternative method to achieve the same behavior change without using fsutil.
This command creates an alias for fsutil.exe and uses it to set the SymlinkEvaluation behavior. It demonstrates a bypass by using an alias instead of the original executable name.
